title: H4 regime-conditional methodology decision
source: [phase5/SPIKE_REPORT.md](phase5/SPIKE_REPORT.md) §"H4 extension" — the spike's deepest empirical finding: M3 v3 + standard walk-forward is structurally biased toward many-event strategies and systematically rejects regime-conditional intraday work *regardless of edge quality*. Reinforced by [phase5/B0030_ARCHIVE.md](phase5/B0030_ARCHIVE.md).
why: H4 BULLQ-MEANREV-002 falsified via `FOLD_CONSTRUCTION_FAILED` not edge-absence. The strategy was non-evaluable, not unprofitable. Three options exist, none free: (a) drop regime gating — abandon the regime-conditional claim, (b) less selective primary — abandon the conjunction discipline that catches shape-leakage, (c) revise audit thresholds for H4 — itself requires v3.2 audit work + DA review. Picking (a/b/c) before B0007/B0008 risks methodology contamination; deferring forever means H4 is silently out of project scope.
value_rationale: M (downgraded from H in 2026-05-27 recalibration — B0032 is the immediate path to H4 evaluability via patch; B0010's deeper methodology question fires only if B0032's chosen patch demonstrably can't handle 200-700 event regime gates without distortion)
scope: a spec document at `docs/superpowers/specs/<date>-h4-regime-conditional-scope.md` that surfaces the trade-off explicitly: enumerate strategy archetypes by event-density, document which the current pipeline can / cannot evaluate, and pre-register the decision. No code in this entry — the spec is the deliverable.
